rubric_items.reasoning: The agent addressed all four root-cause defects (image tag, serviceAccountName, memory unit format, missing 'pods' RBAC verb) rather than papering over symptoms. Because Job pod templates are immutable, deleting and recreating the Job with the corrected spec was the correct way to apply the image/SA/memory fixes, and the RBAC gap was closed with a scoped `kubectl patch` adding just the missing resource. The job reached STATUS=Complete, COMPLETIONS=1/1, with a real Pi value and exit code 0 in the logs — a durable, restart-safe pass, not a lucky one.

The visible trace shows a coherent progression: apply the corrected manifest, wait for completion (first via a backgrounded `kubectl wait`, then direct polling), inspect pod/job status and logs, and conclude only once `succeeded=1` was observed. No contradictory or wasted actions are evident, though the agent polled status multiple times in quick succession before the job actually finished, a minor inefficiency rather than a detour.

Tooling was targeted and least-privilege: a scoped `kubectl patch --type=json` to add just the missing 'pods' resource to the Role (matching the suggested fix exactly), and delete+recreate of the Job (necessary since Job pod templates can't be patched in place) rather than any broader or riskier mutation. Status/log/wait commands were read-only and appropriately scoped to the spark-pi namespace and label selector.

Only 3 mutations total (job delete, job recreate, one RBAC patch) — the minimum needed to fix all four listed issues, with no unrelated resources touched (API activity confined to jobs/pods/roles/serviceaccounts/namespaces in the single spark-pi namespace). No collateral changes or over-engineering.
